metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A Mesoamerican city where Sun-Jaguar Step Pyramid of the Jade Basin, Sacred Grove Beacon, Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Itzamal-Ka should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Itzamal-Ka organizes power through Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court, public response through Sacred Grove Beacon, civic memory through Sun-Jaguar Step Pyramid of the Jade Basin, and daily life through S1 Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, S2 Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, S3 Records / Archives Hall, S4 Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, S5 High Residential Civic Hall.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Limestone Blocks, Jade Tile, Obsidian, Canvas, Turquoise Inlay, Forest, Root Network, Old Growth Woodland, Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Celestial Projection, and exact anchors including Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court, Sacred Grove Beacon, Sun-Jaguar Step Pyramid of the Jade Basin, Manor Court, Temple Platform, Guildhall, Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, Hanging Gardens Monument, Living Canopy Hall Nook, Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A Mesoamerican civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: Sun-Jaguar Step Pyramid of the Jade Basin, Sacred Grove Beacon, Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court, and section anchors such as Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, Records / Archives Hall, Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, High Residential Civic Hall.

canonLocks: Itzamal-Ka uses city code ITK.
The Wonder is Sun-Jaguar Step Pyramid of the Jade Basin.
The Beacon is Sacred Grove Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court and occupies District 13.
The city uses five sections with five tactical district slots per section.

The source-backed section spine is: S1 Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, S2 Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, S3 Records / Archives Hall, S4 Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, S5 High Residential Civic Hall.

rejectedReads: Do not treat Itzamal-Ka as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.
Do not collapse the five-section board into a single mood or single district.
Do not replace exact anchor names with generic summaries.
Do not invent missing street-section landmarks when a street source is absent.
cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.
If an image appears to deviate from a sidecar, preserve the exact name but avoid overclaiming visual details not supported by the source package.
Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.

environment.environmentalLogic: The environment should make Itzamal-Ka's exact civic anchors plausible at street scale: terrain routes people toward section landmarks, lighting clarifies public authority, and water or landscape features remain source-backed rather than decorative filler.

streets.appearance: Streets should show Itzamal-Ka's exact anchor system at pedestrian scale: S1 Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, S2 Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, S3 Records / Archives Hall, S4 Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, S5 High Residential Civic Hall. Keep roads, plazas, stairs, thresholds, lamps, signs-without-readable-text, props, and civilians aligned to the source-backed materials and landmarks.
streets.movement: Movement should be readable for tactical-board assets: citizens pass between 25 district slots, section anchors, D13, service streets, and landmark approaches without visual clutter hiding the civic purpose.
sounds: landmark-specific sounds from water, bells, animals, machines, or crowds when source-backed
smells: worn stone, timber, metal, plaster, or fabric
streets.lighting: Use the package's source-backed civic lighting, daylight/golden-hour reads, lanterns, and beacon glow. Lighting must help identify authority, safety, and landmark function.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce the local terrain and materials without overwhelming the city identity or inventing unsupported climate extremes.
streets.dayCycle: Day scenes emphasize errands, trade, records, repairs, rituals, food, and section-level movement.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes emphasize lantern routes, closed thresholds, guarded landmarks, quiet work, and beacon visibility.

common: street services tied to section anchors

imports: dyes, oils, spices, timber, stone, metal, glass, or cloth as needed by the architecture

housing.wealthy: Prestige housing clusters near Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court, high terraces, or landmark-facing routes, using the best source-backed materials and controlled views of civic space.
housing.middle: Shop-over-house units, courtyard homes, guild-adjacent apartments, and family rooms near section anchors.
housing.poor: Smaller back-street rooms, work-adjacent housing, shared courtyards, service alleys, patched materials, and dense but still city-specific lived-in detail.
